hero.title: Backend engineering with operational discipline
hero.subtitle: I design and ship resilient .NET systems with architecture contracts, quality gates, and rollback plans that work under pressure.

identity.title: Identity / role
paragraphs: I work on API and backend domains where uptime, ownership, and maintainable boundaries matter as much as feature speed.
My delivery model is architecture-first: define contracts, monitor the system, enforce quality checks, and release with an explicit rollback path.
identity.availability: Open to backend architecture and platform reliability engagements

principles.title: Contract-first development
principles.statement: Every module starts with explicit input/output contracts to keep changes local and testable.

principles.title: Observable by default
principles.statement: Telemetry and diagnostics are part of the feature definition, not an afterthought.

principles.title: Rollback-ready releases
principles.statement: Delivery plans include rollout checks, non-regression gates, and fast recovery paths.

items.title: Html2Pdf
items.description: Template-driven HTML to PDF library for .NET with browser pooling and dependency-injection friendly setup.

items.title: SimpleMediator
items.description: Low-boilerplate Mediator/CQRS package focused on modular architecture and reduced coupling.

caseStudy.title: Case study: controlled rollout for portfolio v2
caseStudy.context: A coexistence rollout where v1 remains canonical while v2 is exposed safely.
caseStudy.challenge: Ship a radically different route and UI system without breaking SEO policy, accessibility, or performance budgets.
approach: Centralize content validation in a typed schema and fail fast on locale parity.
Compose route pages from one manifest-driven section model.
Harden rollout with metadata, a11y, and performance guard tests.
outcome: v1 remains stable while v2 evolves independently.

decisionLog.title: Adopt route composition manifests
decisionLog.context: Hardcoded per-route JSX drifted quickly and duplicated logic.
decisionLog.decision: Use a single typed section model and per-route manifests for composition.
decisionLog.tradeoff: Higher upfront typing effort, but deterministic consistency across routes.

decisionLog.title: Keep composition server-side
decisionLog.context: Client toggling would send unnecessary payload and add hydration complexity.
decisionLog.decision: Render only route-allowed sections in server components.
decisionLog.tradeoff: Slightly more route wiring, but stronger SEO semantics and lower runtime complexity.

stackProtocols.title: Schema gate
rules: Block release when EN/ES parity breaks

notes.title: Architecture before implementation
notes.body: The fastest code path is usually the most expensive one to maintain. We define contracts first.

notes.title: AI is an execution tool
notes.body: AI helps with speed, but responsibility for architecture, verification, and release decisions stays human.

contact.title: Contact protocol
contact.description: If you need backend architecture and delivery with explicit quality standards, let’s work together.

seo.title: Kevin Martinez | Portfolio v2
seo.description: Portfolio v2 of Kevin Martinez: backend engineering, modular architecture, and production-grade delivery.
